title: Late Morning
commentary: Full capitulation mode as oil approaches $96—WTI now +9.6%, on pace for one of the biggest single-day gains in a decade. Equities in free-fall: Dow -1.8%, S&P -1.2%, Nasdaq -1.5% with breadth catastrophic at 4:1 decliners. Crypto's safe-haven narrative shattering as BTC dumps -1.6% to $69.5K, ETH -1.0%—the correlation trade is back. Gold surprisingly weak at -0.4% as dollar strength (+0.4%) overwhelms inflation hedging. Fear is the only trade working today.

title: Mid-Morning
commentary: Risk-off accelerating as oil's parabolic move crushes sentiment—WTI now +8.4% at $94.58, the biggest two-day surge in years as Middle East escalation fears grip markets. Dow leading the carnage at -1.8%, S&P -1.2%, Nasdaq -1.3% with broad-based selling across all sectors. Crypto losing its safe-haven bid with BTC slipping to $69.9K (-0.4%) while silver +1.7% outperforms as inflation hedge. Dollar firming to 99.5 as flight to safety intensifies; no dip-buyers in sight.

title: Market Open
commentary: Wednesday opens with oil stealing the show again—WTI +6.5% to $92.88 as Middle East tensions intensify, erasing any peace premium from Monday's crash. Silver surging +2.8% leading metals higher while equities split: Nasdaq +0.1% outperforming as tech catches a bid, but Dow -0.7% dragged by rate-sensitive names. Crypto maintaining momentum with BTC +1.5% near $70.4K and ETH +2.1% showing relative strength. Dollar steady near 99.5 as traders await fresh catalysts.

title: Market Close
commentary: Wall Street closes Tuesday's session lower amid tariff uncertainty—S&P -0.3%, Dow -0.7% as trade policy whiplash continues. Oil reclaiming +5.3% to $87.89 on fresh Middle East jitters, nearly reversing Monday's historic crash. Crypto decoupling from equities with BTC +0.8% at $70,622 and ETH +1.9% showing renewed strength. Silver the big loser at -3.4% as precious metals cool off; dollar firming +0.4% adding pressure. Meta's custom AI chip announcement failing to lift tech sentiment as tariff headlines dominate.

title: Late Session
commentary: Stocks sliding to multi-month lows heading into the close—S&P -0.9%, Dow -1.1% with financials and small caps bearing the brunt. New lows expanding to 163 as sellers maintain control; 65% of stocks now below their 50-day. Crypto catching a bid as BTC +0.9%, ETH +1.8% decouple from the equity bloodbath. Oil still +6.8% keeping energy the sole bright spot. One hour to the bell and no signs of a late-day rescue.

title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Selling pressure persists into the afternoon—Dow -1.0% leading losses as Middle East fears override the tame CPI print. Oil now +7.2% as crude claws back nearly two-thirds of Monday's historic plunge. Breadth worsening to 1:2 with new lows expanding to 156; defensive rotation accelerating. Bitcoin holding $70.7K, remarkably resilient given the equity carnage. Two hours to close and dip-buyers nowhere in sight.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Iran tensions escalating, driving aggressive risk-off rotation—Dow -0.9% leading declines with small caps getting hammered while Nasdaq -0.5% outperforms on Oracle's blowout AI cloud numbers. Oil now +6.5% as geopolitical risk premium roars back; energy the only green sector. Breadth still 2:1 negative with 154 new lows vs 70 new highs. Bitcoin stabilizing near $70.5K as crypto shrugs off equity weakness. FOMC speakers later may add fuel to this fire.

title: Midday
commentary: Selling intensifying into lunch—S&P now -0.6% as sticky inflation narrative lifts 10Y yields and hammers rate-sensitive sectors. Breadth ugly at 2:1 decliners; new lows outpacing highs 2-to-1. Oracle +5% on AI cloud momentum the lone bright spot in tech. Oil surging +5.8% on renewed Middle East tensions, erasing more of Monday's crash. Bitcoin slipping back below $70.3K as risk-off takes hold; gold miners crushed with HMY -12% post-earnings.

title: Mid-Morning
commentary: Equities stabilizing as CPI digestion continues—S&P paring losses to -0.2%, breadth improving with advancers now at 45%. Tech finding footing on AI tailwinds while energy whipsaws +4% as oil rebound accelerates. Bitcoin grinding higher to $70,685 as crypto decouples from equity weakness. Gold miners getting crushed (HMY -10%) despite metal holding gains; sector rotation favoring defensives over cyclicals. Volume picking up ahead of FOMC speakers this afternoon.

title: Morning Session
commentary: Markets trade mixed as CPI fails to clarify Fed's rate-cut timeline—S&P -0.4%, Nasdaq outperforming at -0.2% as AI names catch a bid. Oil rebounding +3.2% on fresh geopolitical jitters, unwinding some of yesterday's historic crash. Bitcoin reclaiming $70K handle (+0.5%) while breadth remains ugly with decliners swamping advancers 3:2. Defensive rotation underway with utilities and staples holding green; energy whipsawing on headline risk.
